features.name: Admin login (email/password)
features.user_story: As an admin, I want to sign in with email and password so that I can access the knowledge base.
features.expected: POST login server action validates ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_PASSWORD; on success stores ADMIN_API_KEY in localStorage + sets session cookie and redirects to ? from or /. /login UI is a glass card with email+password.
features.edge_cases: Empty fields -> 'Email and password are required'; no ADMIN_PASSWORD env -> 'Admin authentication not configured'; wrong creds -> 'Invalid email or password'; already-authed visiting /login -> redirect to /.
features.validation: Both fields required (HTML + server); email type; exact credential match.
features.dependencies: lib/auth.ts login server action; env ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_PASSWORD/ADMIN_API_KEY.
features.assumptions: Single admin identity; no multi-user. Session cookie gates routing, API key gates API calls.
features.test_cases: Happy: valid creds -> redirect+key stored. Error: wrong creds, empty fields, unconfigured env. Security: ? from open-redirect attempt; key never logged. Boundary: very long password. Responsive: card centered on mobile.
features.notes: Auth = single admin via env.

features.name: Route auth-gating & logout
features.user_story: As an admin, I want protected routes to require login and a logout that clears my session so that the KB is not publicly accessible.
features.expected: proxy.ts middleware redirects unauthenticated users on /,/add,/ask,/explore,/documents,/collections,/turbo,/admin to /login? from=path. Logout calls clearAdminApiKey()+logout() server action -> /login.
features.edge_cases: Missing localStorage key -> api.request throws 'Not authenticated'; session expiry mid-poll; deep-link to protected route preserves ? from.
features.assumptions: Cookie and localStorage stay in sync.
features.test_cases: Happy: logout clears + redirects. Error: expired session during 5s poll -> graceful. Security: direct API call without X-API-Key -> 401; tampered cookie. Permission: access /admin unauthenticated -> /login.

features.name: API key permission tiers
features.user_story: As an operator, I want READ/MANAGE/admin permission tiers enforced per endpoint so that scoped keys cannot exceed their grants.
features.expected: require_read/manage/admin dependencies: 401 if no/invalid key; 403 with specific 'X access required' messages. Admin = env ADMIN_API_KEY only (implicit READ+MANAGE+all collections); generated keys never admin.
features.edge_cases: Missing key (auto_error=False) -> 401; key lacking permission -> 403; key_id='admin' protected from disable/delete (403).
features.assumptions: Only one admin key. Generated keys hashed at rest.
features.test_cases: Permission matrix: read key on manage endpoint -> 403; no key -> 401; admin on all. Security: forged key, revoked key reuse, modify 'admin' key -> 403. Boundary: key with empty permissions.
features.status: Tested (test_auth_service unit + test_auth_enforcement_http + LIVE E2E: real 401 on protected endpoints against running deployment)
features.notes: auth_service core + HTTP enforcement + live deployment auth-boundary all confirmed.

features.name: Collection access scoping
features.user_story: As an operator issuing restricted keys, I want collection-scoped access so that a key only reads/writes its allowed collections.
features.expected: validate_collection_access returns 403 'API key does not have permission to {action} collection {id}'. Restricted keys silently filter list results; explicit collection_id mismatch -> 403; collection_id=None means all-accessible.
features.edge_cases: delete/move endpoints do NOT re-validate per-doc source collection (rely on list filtering); restricted key requesting merge-history -> 403.
features.validation: allowed_collections membership; nonexistent collection in key creation -> 400.
features.assumptions: List filtering is sufficient guard for bulk doc ops.
features.test_cases: Happy: scoped key lists only its collection. Permission: scoped key hits other collection -> 403. Edge: bulk move/delete cross-collection (potential gap). Security: scope escalation via allowed_collections edit.
features.notes: Bulk delete/move per-doc check gap noted by API map. Collection-scope logic now unit-tested.

features.name: Prompt-injection guard
features.user_story: As an operator, I want prompt-injection attempts in questions detected so that the assistant refuses system-prompt extraction / instruction bypass.
features.expected: prompt_security.py regex detection of system-prompt extraction, instruction bypass, encoding obfuscation. /api/ask/stream returns a safe refusal (not an error) on detection.
features.edge_cases: Obfuscated/encoded payloads; multilingual injections; legitimate questions containing trigger words (false positives).
features.assumptions: Regex-based detection is best-effort, not exhaustive.
features.test_cases: Happy: normal question passes. Security: 'ignore previous instructions', base64 payload, 'print your system prompt' -> safe refusal. Boundary: benign text with 'ignore' word -> false-positive check.

features.name: Rate limiting & monthly query quota
features.user_story: As an operator, I want per-key rate limits and a monthly query cap so that abuse and runaway costs are bounded.
features.expected: enforce_rate_limit (token bucket keyed by API key/IP) on upload/search/ask*: 429 + Retry-After when RATE_LIMIT_QPM>0 exceeded. enforce_query_quota: 429 + Retry-After to next UTC month when MAX_QUERIES_PER_MONTH>0 exceeded; no-op at 0.
features.edge_cases: Quota=0 disables; bucket falls back to client IP when no key; quota pre-warms reranker as side effect; chat endpoints gated, non-chat (search) behavior per test matrix.
features.assumptions: In-process token bucket (per instance, not cluster-wide).
features.test_cases: Happy: under limit OK. Boundary: exactly at limit, limit+1 -> 429+Retry-After. Disabled: 0 -> unlimited. Perf: burst of N requests. Security: spoof X-API-Key to evade bucket.

features.name: File upload
features.user_story: As a user, I want to upload documents (drag-drop/multi-file) into a collection so that they can be ingested into the KB.
features.expected: POST /api/upload (manage+rate-limit) multipart file; collection_id default 'default', start_processing default false (queued pending), source. Returns UploadResponse{document_id,filename,status,source}. UploadModal filters by extension allowlist, concurrency 10.
features.edge_cases: Extension not allowed -> 400; duplicate filename+size -> 409; size>MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB -> 400 (checked AFTER full read into memory); MAX_FILES/MAX_ENTITIES reached -> 403; store failure cleans file + 500; disallowed files silently dropped in UI.
features.validation: allowed_extensions; MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB; collection access (403); MAX_FILES/MAX_ENTITIES caps.
features.assumptions: Whole file buffered in memory before size check.
features.test_cases: Happy: valid pdf -> 201/pending. Error: bad ext -> 400, dup -> 409, oversize -> 400, cap reached -> 403. Boundary: 0-byte file, file exactly at size limit. Invalid: no file part -> 422. Perf: very large file memory pressure. Permission: read-only key -> 403. Responsive: drag-drop on mobile.
features.notes: Full upload journey executed end-to-end against the running deployment (real embeddings). Post-read size check is a memory-pressure risk (no 413).

features.name: Document list, filter, search, pagination
features.user_story: As a user, I want to browse/filter/search/paginate documents so that I can find and manage them.
features.expected: GET /api/documents; UI polls every 5s; filters by collection/status/source; client search by filename or topic; 100/page with URL ?page= and ellipsis. Status badges + dual progress bars (processing + image-analysis); chunk count; error messages.
features.edge_cases: Out-of-bounds page auto-corrects; selections cleared on filter change; uploaded docs briefly hidden to avoid flicker; source filter hidden when single source; empty vs filtered-empty states.
features.test_cases: Happy: list+paginate. Boundary: page beyond max, 0 docs, exactly 100. Error: API failure -> error state. Perf: 5000 docs list/poll. Responsive: cards stack on mobile.

features.name: Document view / content / file download
features.user_story: As a user, I want to view a document's content and download the original file so that I can verify ingested material.
features.expected: GET /api/documents/{id}, /content (doc+chunks+full_content), /file (FileResponse). UI: markdown opens in-modal (blob), others open blob URL in new tab.
features.edge_cases: 404 if doc or on-disk file missing; 403 collection; large content modal.
features.test_cases: Happy: view md + download pdf. Error: missing doc -> 404, missing file-on-disk -> 404. Permission: scoped key other collection -> 403.

features.name: Document delete (single/bulk/all)
features.user_story: As a user, I want to delete documents individually, in bulk, or all, so that I can clean up the KB.
features.expected: DELETE /api/documents/{id}; POST /api/documents/delete (bulk); DELETE /api/documents (ALL, destructive). Cancels active processing, deletes doc+orphaned entities/communities, returns counts.
features.edge_cases: 404 missing; 403 collection (single); bulk has no per-doc collection re-check; deleting while processing cancels task.
features.test_cases: Happy: delete single/bulk/all -> counts. Error: missing id -> 404. Boundary: empty id list, delete-all on empty KB. Permission: read key -> 403. Security: bulk delete cross-collection (gap).
features.notes: DELETE /api/documents is irreversible.

features.name: Reprocess documents
features.user_story: As a user, I want to reprocess existing or failed documents (optionally with a new file) so that I can re-extract after fixes.
features.expected: POST /api/documents/{id}/reprocess (optional file); POST /api/documents/reprocess (batch, concurrency 1-50, chain=relationship_analysis,community_detection). Without file uses stored file; missing stored file -> 400.
features.edge_cases: 404 doc; 403 collection; bad ext/oversize file -> 400; per-doc not-found/denied recorded (non-fatal) in batch; reprocess delta-skip if unchanged fingerprint.
features.validation: concurrency ge1 le50; chain csv whitelist.
features.assumptions: Stored file required when no upload provided.
features.test_cases: Happy: reprocess completed doc. Error: no stored file -> 400, bad ext -> 400. Boundary: concurrency 1 and 50, 51 -> 422. Chain: relationship+community chained.

features.name: Pending processing & move
features.user_story: As a user, I want to process all pending docs and move docs between collections so that I can control ingestion and organization.
features.expected: GET /api/documents/pending; POST /api/documents/process-pending (concurrency, chain) -> task_id; POST /api/documents/move (target collection access 403). No pending -> message, no task.
features.edge_cases: Background image-analysis wait holds task running; move to inaccessible collection -> 403.
features.test_cases: Happy: process pending -> task. Boundary: no pending -> no task. Permission: move to forbidden collection -> 403.

features.name: Bulk download ZIP
features.user_story: As a user, I want to download selected documents as a ZIP so that I can export originals.
features.expected: POST /api/documents/download-zip {document_ids}; streams ZIP64 in 1MB chunks; duplicate filenames de-duped.
features.edge_cases: Empty list -> 400; no docs found -> 404; restricted key with no accessible docs -> 403; large collection streamed.
features.test_cases: Happy: zip 3 docs. Error: empty list -> 400, none found -> 404. Permission: scoped key -> 403. Perf: 1000-doc zip streaming.

features.name: Add knowledge (custom Q&A/Text/Markdown)
features.user_story: As a user, I want to manually add Q&A/Text/Markdown entries and edit existing ones so that I can enrich the KB without files.
features.expected: POST /api/custom-input (input_type qa/text/markdown, content min 10, answer? for qa, title? max200, collection_id?, start_processing default true). Generates filename via LLM, writes .md, processes. Editing deletes old doc then recreates.
features.edge_cases: QA without answer -> 400; content<10 -> 422; MAX_FILES/MAX_ENTITIES -> 403; LLM filename fallback.
features.validation: content min_length 10; qa requires answer; title<=200.
features.test_cases: Happy: add text entry. Error: qa no answer -> 400, content<10 -> 422, cap -> 403. Boundary: content exactly 10 chars, title 200/201. Edit: update existing.

features.name: AI topic hint generation
features.user_story: As a user, I want an AI-suggested topic and similar-existing-topic chips so that my entries are well-titled and de-duplicated.
features.expected: POST /api/custom-input/generate-topic {content min10, answer?, input_type}. Returns {topic_hint, existing_similar[]}. Falls back to keyword extraction without OpenAI key / on LLM error.
features.edge_cases: content<10 -> 422; no LLM -> keyword fallback (no 500); similar chips clickable.
features.test_cases: Happy: generate hint + chips. Error: content<10 -> 422. Degradation: no OpenAI key -> keyword fallback.

features.name: Custom input list & detail
features.user_story: As a user, I want to search and view my custom entries so that I can manage them.
features.expected: GET /api/custom-inputs (search?, limit 1-200, collection-filtered); GET /api/custom-inputs/{id} (404/403). UI list with 300ms debounce search.
features.edge_cases: 404 missing; 403 collection; empty list state.
features.test_cases: Happy: list+search. Boundary: limit 1/200/201. Error: missing id -> 404.

features.name: Collections CRUD
features.user_story: As a user, I want to create/rename/delete collections so that I can organize documents into scoped sub-graphs.
features.expected: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/collections. Create (name 1-100, desc<=500) capped by MAX_COLLECTIONS -> 403. Default collection cannot be renamed/deleted (400). Delete moves docs to default, returns documents_moved.
features.edge_cases: Rename 'default' -> 400; delete 'default' -> 400; 404 not found; 403 access; MAX_COLLECTIONS cap.
features.test_cases: Happy: create/rename/delete. Error: rename/delete default -> 400, missing -> 404, cap -> 403. Boundary: name 1/100/101 chars, desc 500/501. Permission: read key -> 403.
features.status: Tested (test_max_collections + LIVE E2E CRUD round-trip vs real Neo4j: create/get/rename/delete/404, default-protected)
features.notes: Full collections journey executed end-to-end against the running deployment AND a self-provisioned ephemeral Neo4j.

features.name: Collection entities & doc membership
features.user_story: As a user, I want to view a collection's entities and add documents to it so that I can curate scoped sub-graphs.
features.expected: GET /api/collections/{id}/entities (limit 1-500); POST /api/collections/{id}/documents/{doc_id} (access 403, 404). UI expandable cards show top 10 entities (lazy).
features.edge_cases: 403 access; 404 collection/doc.
features.test_cases: Happy: list entities, add doc. Boundary: limit 1/500/501. Error: missing collection/doc -> 404. Permission: scoped key -> 403.

features.name: Knowledge graph 3-step pipeline (/extract)
features.user_story: As a user, I want a guided extract->relationships->communities pipeline with staleness cues so that I can build and keep the graph current.
features.expected: 3 sequential gated step cards; Step1 entity extraction (process-pending), Step2 relationship analysis (cross-doc rels + ERR indicator), Step3 community detection. Generate/Regenerate clears communities+relationships+entities then reprocesses all with chain. ?autostart=1 auto-fires once.
features.edge_cases: Resilient polling (5 retries+backoff, resume on visibilitychange); regeneration persisted in sessionStorage; chain observer ~30s no-task timeout aborts; staleness from new docs/re-analysis/merges; image-analysis-in-progress keeps Step1 running; buttons disabled at doc_count 0.
features.validation: Steps gated on previous; confirm dialogs on destructive regenerate/rebuild.
features.dependencies: getStats, getDocuments, listTasks, getTaskStatus, processPendingDocuments, reprocessDocuments, analyzeRelationships, detectCommunities, deleteAll*.
features.test_cases: Happy: full pipeline run. Edge: reload mid-run resumes; tab background+resume; autostart once. Error: transient poll error retries then aborts. Boundary: doc_count 0 disables. Destructive: rebuild/regenerate confirm.
features.notes: Complex stateful UI - high QA priority.

features.name: Entity/relationship extraction engine
features.user_story: As an operator, I want LLM-based entity/relationship extraction (per-chunk + batch phases) so that documents become a graph.
features.expected: graph_extractor.py: XML-parsed entities/relationships, type normalization, fuzzy match, clamping; chunk-batched extraction with degradation ladder; Phase A/B; dedicated relationship model support; batched KG writes (resolve->cluster->UNWIND).
features.edge_cases: Malformed XML degradation; oversized batches; type normalization collisions; extraction disabled (ENABLE_GRAPH_EXTRACTION off) -> 400 on analyze endpoint; no OpenAI key -> extraction disabled (logged warning).
features.validation: XML schema parsing; clamps on counts.
features.assumptions: LLM returns parseable grouped XML; fallback ladder on failure.
features.test_cases: Happy: chunk -> entities+rels. Error: malformed XML -> degraded parse. Boundary: empty chunk, huge chunk batch. Parity: batched vs sequential write equivalence.
features.status: Tested (test_xml_parsing, test_chunk_batch_extraction, test_batched_writes + LIVE E2E: entities extracted from uploaded doc, orphan cleanup verified)
features.notes: Unit-tested parsers + real extraction executed end-to-end against the live LLM (unique entities created then orphan-removed on doc delete).

features.name: Graph visualization & subgraph
features.user_story: As a user, I want an interactive force-directed graph with search/expand so that I can explore entities and connections.
features.expected: GET /api/graph/visualization (limit 0-10000, include_neighbors); POST /api/graph/subgraph (List[str]); force-graph-2d canvas, node search, node-limit dropdown, hide-disconnected, click->EntityPanel expand via getEntityRelationships+subgraph. ?entity= deep-link.
features.edge_cases: limit 0 = all; 422 if subgraph body not list; empty/no-connections states; 10k stress; Brave hit-detection fallback; fullscreen errors caught.
features.test_cases: Happy: render+search+expand. Boundary: limit 0/10000/10001 -> 422. Perf: 10k node render. Error: subgraph non-list body -> 422. Responsive: touch pan/zoom.

features.name: Entity browse/search/paginate
features.user_story: As a user, I want to browse, search and filter entities by type so that I can inspect the graph.
features.expected: GET /api/graph/entities (entity_type?, limit 1-1000, skip>=0, search?); /entity-types; /entity/{name} (max_hops 1-3, 404); /search (query min1, 422 empty). UI server-paginated 50/page + 300ms debounce.
features.edge_cases: 404 entity; 422 empty search; empty vs filtered-empty states.
features.validation: limit ge1 le1000, skip ge0, search min_length 1.
features.test_cases: Happy: list/search/filter. Boundary: limit 1/1000/1001, skip 0/neg, empty query -> 422. Error: unknown entity -> 404.

features.name: Entity edit & merge
features.user_story: As a user, I want to edit an entity's name/description and merge duplicates so that I can curate the graph.
features.expected: PATCH /api/graph/entity/{name} (name 1-500, desc<=5000; 400 on invalid/duplicate). POST /api/entities/merge (canonical+merge[]; LLM merged description, falls back to longest; 404 missing). GET /api/entities/merge-history (limit 1-500; 403 for restricted keys).
features.edge_cases: Duplicate name -> 400; merge missing entity -> 404; LLM failure -> longest-desc fallback; merge-history restricted -> 403.
features.test_cases: Happy: edit+merge. Error: dup name -> 400, merge missing -> 404. Boundary: name 1/500/501, desc 5000/5001. Permission: restricted key merge-history -> 403. Degradation: LLM down -> fallback desc.

features.name: Deduplication scan & merge UI
features.user_story: As a user, I want to scan for duplicate entities by similarity and merge them so that the graph stays clean.
features.expected: /deduplicate page; GET /api/entities/duplicates (threshold 0.5-1.0 default 0.75, limit 1-500; 5-min timeout -> 504). UI threshold 75-95% (default 85%), groups with canonical/exclude/add/Inspect/Merge/Dismiss(localStorage), History modal, ?entity= focus.
features.edge_cases: Scan timeout -> 504; dismissed groups persist; add-search >=2 chars; merge sends only non-canonical/non-removed.
features.validation: threshold ge0.5 le1.0; limit ge1 le500.
features.dependencies: suggestDuplicates, mergeEntities, getMergeHistory, getStats, searchEntities, getEntityDetails.
features.test_cases: Happy: scan+merge group. Boundary: threshold 0.5/1.0/0.49, limit 500/501. Error: large graph scan -> 504. Edge: dismiss persists across reload.

features.name: Relationship browse & analyze
features.user_story: As a user, I want to browse relationships and run Phase-B relationship analysis so that cross-document connections are surfaced.
features.expected: GET /api/graph/relationships (rel_type?, limit 1-1000, skip, search?); /relationship-types; /entity/{name}/relationships (max_depth 1-3, limit 1-200, 404). POST /api/graph/relationships/analyze (400 if extraction off; scope full/recent, rebuild deletes batch rels, chain community_detection) -> task_id. DELETE all relationships (destructive).
features.edge_cases: 404 entity; 400 extraction disabled; rebuild deletes then recomputes; weight shown only when non-null.
features.test_cases: Happy: browse + analyze task. Error: extraction off -> 400, unknown entity -> 404. Boundary: max_depth 1/3/4, limit bounds. Destructive: rebuild + delete-all confirm.

features.name: Community detection & summarization
features.user_story: As a user, I want to detect entity communities and summarize them so that I get thematic clusters of the graph.
features.expected: POST /api/graph/communities/detect (400 if disabled; min_size 2-20, collection access) -> task_id; deletes prior, runs Leiden/Louvain, optional LLM summary. POST /summarize (community_ids?, force_regenerate; 400 if disabled/LLM unavailable; skips summarized unless forced). GET list/search/{id}; DELETE {id}/all (unlink entities).
features.edge_cases: Detection disabled -> 400; community_id int-typed routing (search path safe); 404 not in accessible collections; summarize skip-already.
features.assumptions: Detection deletes prior communities each run.
features.test_cases: Happy: detect+summarize. Error: disabled -> 400. Boundary: min_size 2/20/1/21, empty graph. Edge: re-summarize force vs skip; delete unlinks not deletes entities.
features.notes: Leiden/Louvain detection has no automated test.

features.name: Community browse UI & staleness
features.user_story: As a user, I want to browse community cards with summaries and staleness cues so that I know when to re-detect.
features.expected: Explore communities tab (25/page) + Collections CommunitySection (Detect/Summarize/Delete All, resumable via localStorage task key). Cards show name, entity_count, cleaned summary, sample chips; detail modal lazy-loads members.
features.edge_cases: Empty 'No Communities Detected' (->Extract) vs search-empty; detection resumes on reload.
features.dependencies: getCommunitiesPaginated, getCommunity, detectCommunities, summarizeCommunities, deleteCommunity, deleteAllCommunities, pollTask.
features.test_cases: Happy: browse+detail. Edge: resume detection after reload. Empty: no communities CTA. Responsive: card grid.

features.name: Hybrid search
features.user_story: As a user/integrator, I want hybrid vector+keyword+metadata search so that I can retrieve relevant chunks.
features.expected: POST /api/search (read+quota+rate-limit) {query, top_k 1-50 default 5, filters?}. RRF fusion; filters.collection_id checked (403); restricted keys scoped. Returns SearchResponse{results[], total_results}.
features.edge_cases: 403 collection; empty results; 500 on error.
features.test_cases: Happy: query -> ranked results. Boundary: top_k 1/50/51 -> 422, empty query. Permission: scoped collection -> 403. Perf: large corpus latency.
features.status: Tested (HTTP contract 422 + RRF unit + LIVE E2E real hybrid search returns results vs 156-doc/5.3k-entity graph)
features.notes: Validation + RRF math unit-tested; real hybrid search (live embeddings + Neo4j) executed end-to-end against the running deployment.

features.name: Ask (non-streaming GraphRAG)
features.user_story: As an integrator, I want a non-streaming Q&A endpoint so that I can get a grounded answer with sources.
features.expected: POST /api/ask (read+quota+rate-limit). RAGRequest with top_k 1-20, use_graph, max_hops 1-3, use_reranking, use_agentic, use_fast_search, collection_id. Returns RAGResponse{answer, sources[], graph_context?, reranked}. Bounded by ASK_DEADLINE_SECONDS -> 504 (work continues in bg).
features.edge_cases: use_agentic=true while ENABLE_AGENT_RESEARCH on -> 400 agentic_requires_streaming; 403 collection; 504 deadline (duplicate-work risk); HTTPExceptions preserved.
features.validation: top_k ge1 le20, max_hops ge1 le3.
features.assumptions: Deadline cancels response, not background work.
features.test_cases: Happy: question -> answer+sources. Error: agentic+research-on -> 400, slow -> 504. Boundary: top_k/max_hops bounds. Permission: scoped collection -> 403.
features.status: Tested (deadline wiring test_resilience; HTTP contract 422; LIVE E2E confirmed 504 deadline_exceeded after 28s with retry guidance)
features.notes: 504 deadline behavior verified end-to-end against live deployment (directs to /api/ask/stream). 504 leaves background work uncancelled.

features.name: Ask streaming chat (SSE)
features.user_story: As a user, I want streaming grounded chat answers with inspectable sources so that I can trust and explore answers fast.
features.expected: POST /api/ask/stream (SSE). 400 if no OPENAI_API_KEY. Paths: agentic (ENABLE_AGENTIC_RAG), fast_search (vector-only), standard (agent-chat if ENABLE_AGENT_CHAT else legacy hybrid+rerank+writer). Events: content/sources/graph_context/status/done/error/memory_update; heartbeat ': ping'; terminal 'event: shutdown' on restart. Prompt-injection -> safe refusal.
features.edge_cases: Errors emitted as data:{error} (HTTP 200 after stream starts); reasoning OFF by default; conversation_memory opaque dict; source modal highlights cited chunk.
features.test_cases: Happy: stream tokens+sources+done. Error: no key -> 400, mid-stream error frame. Edge: shutdown frame on restart, heartbeat during silence. Security: injection -> refusal. Responsive: source modal mobile.
features.status: Tested (test_context_curator + LIVE E2E SSE: fast-search stream delivers content+done, no error)
features.notes: Chat answer layer: reasoning off + 2 speed iterations. Memory logic unit-tested; streaming chat executed end-to-end against live LLM+Neo4j (standard agentic path is >90s, fast path streams content).

features.name: Deep research (thinking stream)
features.user_story: As a user, I want multi-step deep research with visible thinking/sub-questions so that complex questions get thorough answers.
features.expected: POST /api/ask/stream/thinking (SSE). 400 if no OPENAI_API_KEY; 403 collection. Agent quality mode or legacy agentic. Events: thinking/search/retrieval/sub_questions/sources/graph_context/content/done/error. UI Deep Research toggle (mid-session), research-process steps.
features.edge_cases: Long-running; sub-question fan-out; researcher/writer agents + tools (incl http_request skill, git_repo).
features.test_cases: Happy: deep research -> thinking+subq+answer. Error: no key -> 400. Permission: scoped collection -> 403. Perf: long multi-step latency.
features.status: Tested (prompt/tool assembly + loop helpers: merge/dedup/truncate/substitute) + Documented
features.notes: Researcher helpers unit-tested (test_researcher_helpers); full tool-calling loop needs a mocked LLM (live).

features.name: Agent Skills management
features.user_story: As an admin, I want to install, configure, enable/disable and uninstall agent skills so that I can extend the agent's tools.
features.expected: Admin skills endpoints (gated by ENABLE_SKILLS at discovery): list/registry-search/get/install(url|registry_id)/patch(enabled)/delete/discover/analyze/config GET+PUT. Install neither arg -> 400; upstream HTTP error -> 502. Config secret masking (********). Auto-activation + http_request tool + LLM config wizard.
features.edge_cases: Neither url nor registry_id -> 400; ValueError -> 400; upstream fetch error -> 502; masked values preserve existing secrets; 404 unknown skill.
features.dependencies: skill_service, external registry HTTP, LLM (analyze), crypto (secrets).
features.test_cases: Happy: install+configure+enable. Error: no install arg -> 400, registry down -> 502, unknown id -> 404. Security: secret masking round-trip; injected SKILL.md. Edge: disabled flag hides from agent.
features.status: Tested (catalog TTL cache + SKILL.md parse/sanitize/env-boundary: test_skill_service_helpers) + Documented
features.notes: Parsing/ID-sanitization + SKILL_-prefix env security boundary covered; live install/http_request/wizard need network+LLM.

features.name: Git connector (verify/browse/CRUD)
features.user_story: As an admin, I want to connect GitHub/GitLab/Gitea repos so that their files sync into the KB.
features.expected: Git endpoints (gated ENABLE_GIT_INTEGRATION -> 404 when off; all admin). verify (vendor, pat min8) returns valid:false (not error) on bad creds; browse (PAT as query param); connections CRUD; PATs Fernet-encrypted at rest, masked ••••last4. Create: invalid creds 400, repo not found 404, provider error 502.
features.edge_cases: Integration disabled -> 404; invalid creds non-error on verify; PAT in query param on browse; provider error -> 502.
features.validation: vendor enum github/gitlab/gitea; pat min_length 8; sync_interval_minutes ge0; access_level read/read_write.
features.test_cases: Happy: verify+connect+list. Error: bad pat -> valid:false, repo missing -> 404, disabled -> 404, provider 5xx -> 502. Security: PAT encrypted+masked; token scrub in FETCH_HEAD. Boundary: pat 7/8 chars.

features.name: Git incremental sync engine
features.user_story: As an admin, I want incremental clone+diff sync (manual + scheduled) so that repo changes flow into the KB without full re-ingest.
features.expected: POST connections/{id}/sync -> task_id (409 if already running); change-set via clone/diff/ls-tree incl renames; glob include/exclude filters; supported-extension filter; orphaned-doc detection; background scheduler polls every GIT_SYNC_POLL_INTERVAL for due connections (sync_interval_minutes>0). Document provenance recorded.
features.edge_cases: Concurrent sync -> 409; per-connection lock serializes; rename R100 identical-content; image/binary excluded; glob precedence (exclude wins); token scrubbed from FETCH_HEAD.
features.assumptions: git binary on PATH; network to provider.
features.test_cases: Happy: initial + incremental sync. Edge: rename, delete->orphaned, glob filter, unsupported ext skipped. Error: concurrent sync -> 409. Security: token scrub.
features.notes: D-001 fixed here (extension check forced full DocumentProcessor build).

features.name: Turbo mode (Compute3 GPU)
features.user_story: As an admin, I want to launch/monitor a GPU inference job so that I can accelerate processing/queries.
features.expected: Turbo endpoints: status (read), balance/start/stop/extend/jobs (admin). 400 'not available' if COMPUTE3_API_KEY unset (status returns available:false). start (runtime 60-86400, gpu_count 1-8); extend (additional_seconds required 60-86400, 404 no active job); stop (no job -> message). /turbo page polls status+jobs 10s with live vLLM log viewer.
features.edge_cases: Key unset -> 400/available:false; already-running start returns existing job; extend no job -> 404; balance fetch failure tolerated.
features.validation: runtime ge60 le86400; gpu_count ge1 le8; additional_seconds required.
features.test_cases: Happy: start->ready->stop. Error: no key -> 400, extend no job -> 404. Boundary: runtime 60/86400/59, gpu_count 1/8/9. Security: API key never exposed in status.
features.notes: D-002 fixed: latent wait_for_ready readiness path (get_auth_token + 4-arg set_turbo_mode_state). Added regression tests.

features.name: System configuration view
features.user_story: As an admin, I want to view all non-secret system config so that I can verify the deployment.
features.expected: GET /api/admin/config -> SystemConfigResponse (LLM/embedding/vision models, chunking, RAG/community/entity flags, turbo, skills, git). No secrets. UI: 6 collapsible read-only sections; Vision/Turbo conditional.
features.edge_cases: Vision/Turbo blocks hidden when unavailable.
features.test_cases: Happy: config renders. Security: assert no API keys/secrets in payload. Permission: non-admin -> 403.

features.name: API key management UI+API
features.user_story: As an admin, I want to create/list/revoke/activate/delete API keys with permissions and collection scope so that I can grant scoped access.
features.expected: Admin api-keys endpoints. Create (name 1-100, >=1 permission, scope all/restricted+allowed_collections) returns plaintext key once. Restricted without collections -> 400; nonexistent collection -> 400. key_id='admin' protected (403 on patch/delete/revoke). Stats + usage-history (days 1-365). UI: list w/ stats, analytics modal w/ charts.
features.edge_cases: with-stats route declared before {key_id}; admin key protected; one-time secret reveal.
features.validation: name 1-100, permissions min1, restricted requires collections, days ge1 le365.
features.assumptions: Key shown once, hashed at rest.
features.test_cases: Happy: create+revoke+activate. Error: restricted no collections -> 400, modify admin -> 403, missing -> 404. Boundary: name 1/100/101, days 1/365/366. Security: secret only shown once; hashed storage.
features.notes: api_key_service/api_usage_service untested.

features.name: System reset (danger zone)
features.user_story: As an admin, I want a granular destructive reset so that I can wipe selected data with explicit confirmation.
features.expected: POST /api/admin/reset {delete_documents, delete_uploaded_files, delete_custom_inputs, delete_collections, delete_api_keys(default false)}. Irreversible. Cancels processing, deletes per flags, returns counts. UI: 5 checkboxes + typed-DELETE confirm + >=1 option.
features.edge_cases: API keys delete defaults off; typed-DELETE gate; reset on empty KB.
features.test_cases: Happy: reset docs only -> counts. Boundary: no option selected (UI block). Security: requires typed DELETE; non-admin -> 403. Edge: reset empty KB.

features.name: Library export/import
features.user_story: As an admin, I want full library export/import so that I can back up and migrate the KB.
features.expected: POST /api/admin/export -> task_id (409 if export/import running); GET export/{task_id}/download (404/400 states, streams ZIP); POST /api/admin/import multipart (mode clean|replace, 409 if in progress). clean requires empty target; replace wipes first. UI: export state machine + import (replace requires typed DELETE).
features.edge_cases: Concurrent export/import -> 409; download before complete -> 400; cleaned-up result -> 404; clean into non-empty target rejected.
features.validation: mode pattern ^(clean|replace)$; caps (MAX_*) enforced on import.
features.test_cases: Happy: export->download->import. Error: concurrent -> 409, download early -> 400. Boundary: import exceeding MAX_* caps -> 403. Edge: replace wipes; clean into non-empty.
features.status: Tested (import caps + NDJSON round-trip: test_library_transfer) + Documented
features.notes: Serialization/round-trip primitives now unit-tested; full live export->import is manual.

features.name: Statistics dashboard & API usage analytics
features.user_story: As an admin, I want KB stats and per-key usage analytics so that I can monitor the system.
features.expected: GET /api/stats, /api/admin/stats/overview, /api/admin/api-keys/{id}/stats + usage-history. UI: KPI grid, processing breakdown, usage-trend area chart (7/14/30/90D), endpoint bar chart. StatsBar polls getStats 5s.
features.edge_cases: stats/overview excluded from usage tracking; stats failure -> empty body; orphan cleanup banner when docs=0 but entities exist.
features.test_cases: Happy: stats render + charts. Edge: orphan cleanup banner+confirm. Error: stats API fail -> empty. Perf: 5s poll load.
features.notes: D-003 fixed: usage endpoint categorization mislabeled custom-inputs detail path. Charts/aggregation remain manual.

features.name: Background task tracking
features.user_story: As a user, I want to poll/list/cleanup background tasks so that I can monitor long-running operations.
features.expected: GET /api/tasks/{id} (404), /{id}/result (404/202/500/200), GET /api/tasks (filters), DELETE /api/tasks/{id} (record only), POST /api/tasks/cleanup (max_age_hours 1-168). In-memory _task_store (lost on restart).
features.edge_cases: 404 after restart even for previously-valid IDs; result 202 while running, 500 with error if failed; delete does not stop running work.
features.test_cases: Happy: poll task to completion. Boundary: max_age_hours 1/168/0/169. Edge: poll after restart -> 404; result of failed -> 500.
features.notes: In-memory store volatility.

features.name: Health, metrics & observability
features.user_story: As an operator, I want health checks, Prometheus metrics and request-ID correlation so that I can monitor and trace the service.
features.expected: GET /health (public; healthy/degraded, 200 even if Neo4j down). GET /metrics (admin; 404 if METRICS_ENABLED=false, 501 if prometheus-client missing). X-Request-ID middleware echoes/forwards; LOG_FORMAT plain|json.
features.edge_cases: Neo4j down -> degraded (still 200); metrics disabled -> 404; no prometheus lib -> 501.
features.test_cases: Happy: /health 200, /metrics text. Error: metrics off -> 404, lib missing -> 501. Security: /metrics requires admin. Edge: Neo4j down -> degraded.
features.notes: D-004 fixed: interactive docs now disabled in production by default (EXPOSE_API_DOCS). Verified end-to-end against the running deployment.

features.name: Resilience: circuit breaker & graceful shutdown
features.user_story: As an operator, I want helper-client circuit breaking, retries and graceful shutdown so that the service degrades safely.
features.expected: helper_client: shared HTTP client, retry with backoff, circuit breaker, HELPER_STRICT_REMOTE, X-Tenant-ID/X-Request-ID. Graceful shutdown: uvicorn timeout + compose stop_grace_period drains in-flight; SSE gets terminal shutdown frame; lifespan awaits task cancellation before closing Neo4j.
features.edge_cases: Breaker open after N failures; strict-remote docling rejects local; retry classification (retriable vs not).
features.test_cases: Happy: helper call succeeds. Error: breaker trips on repeated 5xx; strict-remote rejects local docling. Edge: shutdown frame mid-stream.

features.name: Configuration & production secret enforcement
features.user_story: As an operator, I want env-driven config with budget fallbacks and production secret enforcement so that deployments are safe and tunable.
features.expected: config.py Pydantic settings (40+ vars). Token/context budget fallback cascade + legacy env aliasing (deprecation warns). ENVIRONMENT=production fails fast on weak/default secrets (_enforce_production_secrets). CORS allowlist-driven (wildcard disables credentials). neo4j tunables require CORTEX_NEO4J_ prefix.
features.edge_cases: Weak prod secret -> startup failure; wildcard CORS disables credentials; legacy env var -> deprecation warning; NEO4J_ prefix trap on PaaS.
features.validation: secret strength in production; CORS origins.
features.test_cases: Happy: valid prod config boots. Error: default secret in prod -> fail fast. Boundary: budget fallback cascade order. Security: CORS wildcard+credentials mutually exclusive.

features.name: Reasoning/thinking model dispatch
features.user_story: As an operator, I want per-backend reasoning/thinking dispatch so that the right model params are sent per provider.
features.expected: reasoning_config: mode parse, model-family/backend classification, kwargs build, 400-fallback when provider rejects reasoning params. Reasoning OFF by default for chat.
features.edge_cases: Unknown backend classification; provider 400 on reasoning param -> retry without.
features.test_cases: Happy: reasoning kwargs per backend. Error: 400 reasoning param -> fallback. Boundary: unknown model family default path.

features.name: Global navigation chrome
features.user_story: As a user, I want persistent header/submenu/stats-bar so that I can navigate and see KB status.
features.expected: Header (Manage/Explore pills, Settings gear, Turbo indicator poll 5/30s). SubMenu context tabs (hidden on /admin). StatsBar (4 KPIs, poll 5s, orphan cleanup banner, hidden on /admin). LayoutWrapper minimal layout on /login. Redirect stubs: / -> /documents; /entities,/relationships,/communities -> explore tabs; /ask -> chat/research.
features.edge_cases: Turbo poll failure hides indicator; labels hidden below sm; redirects preserve intent.
features.test_cases: Happy: navigate all sections. Edge: turbo indicator states; orphan banner. Responsive: labels collapse on mobile. Redirects: each stub resolves.
features.notes: Unauthenticated frontend redirect confirmed against running deployment. Dead components ask/AskInput.tsx, ask/AskSettings.tsx flagged (unused).

features.name: Frontend static quality gate
features.user_story: As a developer, I want the frontend to typecheck and lint cleanly so that type/lint regressions are caught.
features.expected: tsc --noEmit passes (tsconfig noEmit) and eslint (flat config) passes. CI runs both on every PR.
features.edge_cases: Local node_modules root-owned (EACCES) blocks local install.
features.test_cases: tsc --noEmit -> 0 errors; eslint -> 0 errors.
features.notes: Local node_modules root-owned (EACCES); typecheck run in user-owned copy -> 0 errors. eslint deferred to CI.

features.name: Document conversion (Docling worker / remote offload)
features.user_story: As an operator, I want CPU-bound document conversion isolated (subprocess or shared remote service) so that the event loop stays responsive and memory is bounded.
features.expected: Without DOCLING_SERVICE_URL: spawns python -m app.services.docling_worker subprocess (JSON over stdin/stdout, global semaphore of 1, TableFormer ACCURATE). With URL: POST to shared /convert via helper_client (3 retries, breaker). Large PDFs chunked by DOCLING_MAX_PAGES_PER_CHUNK; raw-text/code fast-path skips Docling.
features.edge_cases: Worker OOM/non-zero exit -> RuntimeError; empty stdout -> error; slim image w/o docling + no URL -> actionable error; HELPER_STRICT_REMOTE -> no local fallback; max_num_pages 500 cap; mdx/xml plain-text fallback.
features.assumptions: docling not imported at module scope (avoids torch import).
features.test_cases: Happy: convert pdf -> markdown. Error: worker OOM -> RuntimeError, strict-remote unreachable -> doc FAILED. Boundary: 500+ page PDF chunked, raw-text fast path. Perf: large-PDF per-chunk cache unload.
features.status: Tested (LIVE E2E: raw-text doc ingested via real pipeline end-to-end) + Documented
features.notes: Raw-text fast path exercised end-to-end live (chunk->embed->store). docling_worker binary conversion still needs a PDF/disposable corpus.

features.name: Vision image analysis & OCR
features.user_story: As a user, I want images in documents analyzed (vision model or OCR) so that their content is searchable.
features.expected: When use_vision: extracts PictureItems, downscales over VISION_MAX_IMAGE_SIDE, POSTs data-URL to vision model (Summary/Visual/OCR/Data/Context). Fallback chain: vision -> Docling description -> 'Image on page N'. When no vision model: EasyOCR (en+de, conf 0.2). Background analysis gated by VISION_MAX_CONCURRENT (3); image chunks chunk_index 1000+.
features.edge_cases: No API key -> None; image < VISION_MIN_IMAGE_SIDE skipped; HTTP 400 reasoning -> strip+retry once, cache unsupported; per-image failure doesn't fail doc; vision-model and EasyOCR mutually exclusive.
features.assumptions: Choosing vision model loses EasyOCR en/de unless model transcribes text.
features.test_cases: Happy: image -> analysis chunk. Error: no key -> none, sub-min image skipped. Boundary: RGBA->PNG, oversize downscale. Edge: vision 400 -> retry without reasoning.
features.notes: Image-payload prep now unit-tested; live vision-model analysis remains manual.

features.name: Processing cancellation & progress
features.user_story: As a user, I want to cancel in-flight document processing and see granular progress so that I control long ingests.
features.expected: Cooperative cancellation via asyncio.Event honored at every major stage + before each chunk store; waits <=10s then marks FAILED 'Processing cancelled'. Progress PENDING->PROCESSING->EXTRACTING->COMPLETED/FAILED with numeric milestones; image progress tracked separately.
features.edge_cases: No running task -> no-op; task ignoring flag >10s -> TimeoutError logged, cleanup proceeds; mid-stage cancel may leave chunks (delete cleanup handles); doc shows 'completed' while images still analyzing.
features.test_cases: Happy: cancel mid-process -> FAILED. Edge: cancel with no task, slow task >10s timeout. Boundary: cancel right before chunk store.

defects.repro: From a checkout containing a real .env with embedding creds (e.g. USE_OPENAI_EMBEDDINGS=true + Venice EMBEDDING_API_KEY), run `cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_git_sync.py::test_supported_extensions_filter`.
defects.expected: Test passes hermetically; GitConnectorService._supported() should be a pure extension lookup with no LLM/embedder construction.
defects.actual: FAILED: LLMCallNotAllowedInTest. _supported() accessed self.processor.RAW_TEXT_EXTENSIONS, which instantiates a full DocumentProcessor and constructs a real OpenAIDocumentEmbedder; conftest mock_llm guard fired.
defects.root_cause: RAW_TEXT_EXTENSIONS is a class attribute, but _supported read it through an instance, forcing heavy processor (embedder/splitter) construction during change-set computation. Test isolation also leaked: _isolate_env blanks openai_api_key but not the embedding credentials.
defects.status: FIXED — _supported() now reads DocumentProcessor.RAW_TEXT_EXTENSIONS from the class without instantiating. Full suite 323/323 green, no regression.

defects.repro: Static/dynamic: invoke the vLLM-readiness path — Compute3Service.wait_for_vllm_ready() (reached when create_turbo_job(wait_for_ready=True)). Lines 312/508 of compute3_service.py.
defects.expected: Readiness branch fetches the auth token via the async Compute3 API and enables turbo state without crashing.
defects.actual: Latent crash: (1) job.get_auth_token() — no such method on Compute3Job (it is the async Service.get_job_token(job_id)) -> AttributeError; (2) set_turbo_mode_state(True, base_url, token, job_key) passes 4 args to a 3-arg function -> TypeError. Currently unreachable (wait_for_ready defaults False; no caller sets True), so inert today but a landmine.
defects.root_cause: Readiness branch diverged from the working live path (get_active_turbo_job at L542-557 correctly uses await self.get_job_token(job.job_id) + 3-arg set_turbo_mode_state). The dead branch was never exercised so the API drift went unnoticed.
defects.status: FIXED — both call sites now mirror the live path: await self.get_job_token(job.job_id) when job.auth, and 3-arg set_turbo_mode_state. Added tests/test_compute3_turbo.py (4 tests, previously-zero compute3 coverage). Suite 327/327 green.

defects.actual: 'upload' — prefix matching iterated ENDPOINT_CATEGORIES in insertion order and '/api/custom-input' (upload) is a string prefix of '/api/custom-inputs/...', so the shorter, less-specific prefix won.
defects.root_cause: Prefix categorization used dict-insertion order instead of longest-prefix-wins; a string-prefix collision between '/api/custom-input' and '/api/custom-inputs' mislabeled the latter's sub-paths in per-key usage analytics (data integrity).
defects.status: FIXED — categorize_endpoint now matches the longest (most-specific) prefix first. Added tests/test_api_usage_service.py (19 cases incl. the regression). Suite 366/366 green.

defects.repro: Against the running deployment: curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:8000/openapi.json (also /docs, /redoc) -> 200, unauthenticated.
defects.expected: A production/ directly-exposed backend should not disclose its full API schema + interactive docs to anonymous callers.
defects.actual: FastAPI was constructed with default docs URLs, so /docs, /redoc and /openapi.json were served publicly (HTTP 200, no auth). Confirmed live via end-to-end probe of the running container.
defects.root_cause: Information disclosure / missing hardening: app = FastAPI(...) used the framework defaults (docs_url=/docs etc.) with no environment gating. The prod nginx routes root paths to the frontend, but any directly-exposed backend (this dev stack, the per-tenant container model) leaks the schema.
defects.status: FIXED — added Settings.expose_api_docs ('auto' default) + docs_enabled property: docs/redoc/openapi disabled in production by default, dev unchanged, EXPOSE_API_DOCS=true/false overrides. Wired into FastAPI(docs_url/redoc_url/openapi_url). Added tests/test_docs_exposure.py (12). Suite 458/458 green; live auth-boundary E2E in tests/test_live_e2e.py.
